login_error: Login failed — please check your username and password.
login_required: Please enter your username and password.
session_expired_body: You've been signed out — sign in to continue. Your work on this page is kept.
session_expired_fallback: Sign in on the login page instead
month_progress: Worked {pct}% of the target so far
settings_section_account_hint: Saved to your account and applied on every device — press Save to apply changes.
settings_section_device_hint: Stored only in this browser. Changes apply immediately — no Save needed.
settings_min_entry_duration_hint: A new entry's end pre-fills to its start plus this many minutes. 0 disables it.
settings_grid_enter: When editing a table cell, Enter…
settings_grid_enter_hint: Tab always moves to the next cell.
settings_dateformat_pattern_hint: Tokens: YYYY YY MM M DD D (also %Y %m %d). Other characters stay literal. Applies to display only — editing always uses ISO.
settings_dateformat_invalid: Invalid pattern — include a day, month or year token (e.g. DD.MM.YYYY).
settings_font_hint: Body text only; headings stay unchanged.
extras_time_hint: Only used when contract times are disabled.
extras_date_required: Start and end date are required.
extras_date_order: Start date must be before or equal to end date.
extras_time_order: Start time must be before end time.
help_intro: TimeTracker is where you log your work time. The Worklog is a table where each row is one block of time. To get started, add an entry and fill in its customer, project and activity — plus a ticket if you have one.
help_usage_add: Add entry: click "Add entry" or press Alt + A.
help_usage_edit: Edit entry: double-click a cell of an existing entry, or focus it and press Enter.
help_usage_delete: Delete entry: click the trash (delete) icon in its row — or focus the row, Tab to the icon and press Enter.
help_usage_focus: The current row is highlighted; move through entries with the arrow keys.
help_usage_autosave: Saving: a completed row saves on its own; the disk icon saves a row right away, and the reset icon discards its changes.
help_shortcuts_intro: Arrow keys move you around — ←/→ along the menu, ↓ into the page, and cell to cell inside a table. On the Worklog and Administration tables you can edit a cell in place: press Enter or just start typing. Alt+1–7, / and ? work everywhere.
help_sc_search: Jump to the search / filter field
help_sc_row_edges: First / last cell in the row
help_sc_grid_edges: First / last cell in the table
help_sc_page: Move up / down a page of rows
help_sc_enter_cell: Edit the cell in place, or use its control (sort, action)
help_sc_type_edit: Start editing the focused cell by typing into it
help_sc_commit_cell: While editing: save and move down / to the next cell, or cancel
help_sc_leave_cell: Return from a control to the cell
help_sc_search_table: Move from the search field into the table
help_sc_search_clear: Clear the filter, or leave the search field
help_sc_arrow_nav: Move between the menu, sub-menu, search and table
help_sc_info: Show info for the selected/last entry
help_sc_prolong: Prolong the last entry to the current time
help_legend_intro: Each worklog row is colour-coded by how it relates to the entry above it, and the Actions column uses icons. Here is what they mean.
help_legend_daybreak_desc: The first entry of a day — it marks the start of a new working day.
help_legend_break_desc: Starts after the previous entry ended, so there is an unbooked gap (a break) before it.
help_legend_overlap_desc: Starts before the previous entry ended, so the two time ranges overlap.
help_icon_continue_desc: Start a new entry for the same customer, project, activity and ticket, ready for the time you log next.
help_icon_prolong_desc: Set this entry's end time to now, extending the entry you are currently working on.
help_icon_info_desc: Show a summary of the time already booked on this entry's customer, project, activity and ticket.
help_icon_delete_desc: Delete the entry, after a confirmation prompt.
help_icon_save_desc: Save the row's pending changes right away. It appears only while a row has unsaved edits — a completed row otherwise saves on its own.
help_icon_discard_desc: Discard the row's unsaved changes and restore the last saved values. A brand-new, never-saved row is removed.
help_controls_intro: Quick display and account controls — most sit at the top of every page and remember your choice on this device.
help_control_theme_desc: Cycles the colour theme: System (follows your device), then Light, then Dark.
help_control_density_desc: Cycles the spacing: Comfortable, then Compact, then Ultra-compact. Ultra-compact also hides the small hint texts and the worklog legend for a maximum-density view.
help_control_layout_desc: Choose where the menu sits — a top bar or a left sidebar — on the Settings page.
help_control_logout_desc: Sign out of TimeTracker and return to the login page.
help_pages_intro: What each area of TimeTracker is for. Billing and Administration are only visible to project leads and admins.
help_page_tracking_desc: Your worklog: add, edit and delete time entries directly in the grid.
help_page_month_desc: Month calendar with your booked times, contract targets and running balance.
help_page_auswertung_desc: Filterable evaluation of booked effort by customer, project, ticket, activity, user and day.
help_page_billing_desc: Monthly statement: export booked times as an XLSX file, filtered by user, project or customer.
help_page_admin_desc: Manage customers, projects, users, teams, holidays, presets, ticket systems, activities and contracts.
help_page_settings_desc: Personal preferences: language, time suggestions, date format, font, text size and navigation layout.
tracking_empty_range: No entries in the last {count} days.
tracking_invalid_time: Please enter a valid start and end time.
tracking_time_order: Start time must be before end time.
tracking_delete_body: This permanently deletes the selected worklog entry.
tracking_edit_hint: Double-click a cell — or focus it and press Enter — to edit. Tab within a row reaches its actions; Alt+C / Alt+P / Alt+I act on the current row. A completed row saves automatically; the disk icon force-saves an unsaved row and the reset icon discards it.
auswertung_pick_filter: Choose at least a customer, project, team, user, activity, ticket or description.
admin_empty: No entries yet — use “Add” to create one.
admin_reset_2fa_confirm: Reset this user's second factors? This removes their authenticator setup and all passkeys — they will sign in with just their password until they enrol again.
admin_sync_all_confirm: Sync subtickets for all projects? This may take a while.
admin_subtickets_synced_partial: Some projects could not be synced.
admin_desc_customers: Customers you book time for. Global customers are available to all teams; inactive ones aren't offered for booking.
admin_desc_projects: Projects belong to a customer. Inactive projects aren't offered for booking.
admin_desc_users: Staff accounts. Inactive users can't log in and aren't offered as leads — their entries are kept.
admin_desc_teams: Teams group users and customers for evaluations and access.
admin_desc_holidays: Public holidays counted as non-working days in time evaluations.
admin_holiday_import_hint: Import public holidays from an iCal feed URL or an uploaded .ics file. Existing dates are updated, new dates added.
admin_holiday_import_needs_input: Enter a feed URL or choose an .ics file.
admin_desc_presets: Templates for frequent bookings (customer / project / activity / description).
admin_desc_ticketsystems: Ticket systems (Jira) for linking tickets and syncing times.
admin_desc_activities: Activity types (e.g. development, meeting). The factor weights the duration in evaluations.
admin_desc_contracts: Employment contracts with target hours per weekday, for target/actual evaluation.
admin_help_global: Global entries are available to all teams, not just the assigned ones.
admin_help_billing: Billing method: T&M (by effort), fixed price, or none.
admin_help_user_type: Role: USER/DEV (time tracking), PL (project lead — currently includes admin rights), ADMIN (full administration).
admin_help_factor: Weights the duration in evaluations (1.0 = normal).
admin_help_needs_ticket: If on, a ticket number is required for this activity.
admin_help_book_time: If on, bookings are written back to this ticket system.
admin_help_password: Set or replace this user's local password (min. 8 characters). Leave blank to keep the current one. Only used when the authentication source is Local.
admin_help_auth_source: Where this user's password is checked. Local uses a password stored in the timetracker; LDAP uses the directory.
admin_f_ext_info: Pull info from the external ticket system
admin_f_secret_hint: Secrets are write-only — leave blank to keep the stored value.
settings_section_security_hint: Manage your password and two-factor authentication.
settings_password_mismatch: The new passwords do not match.
settings_password_error: The password could not be changed.
settings_2fa_off_hint: Add a second step at sign-in with an authenticator app.
settings_2fa_scan_hint: Add this key to your authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Aegis, 1Password …), then enter the 6-digit code it shows.
settings_2fa_backup_hint: Each code works once, for signing in when your authenticator is unavailable. They are shown only now — store them somewhere safe.
settings_2fa_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
settings_2fa_bad_code: That code wasn't right. Enter a current code from your authenticator app, or a backup code.
settings_2fa_disable_hint: Enter a current code from your authenticator app (or a backup code) to turn two-factor off.
tracking_ticket_link_hint: Ctrl+click (⌘ on Mac) opens the ticket — a plain click edits the cell
twofactor_gate_intro: Your organisation now requires a second sign-in step. Add an authenticator app or a passkey to continue.
login_2fa_hint: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app — or one of your backup codes.
login_2fa_error: That code is not valid. Check your authenticator and try again.
settings_passkey_hint: Sign in with your fingerprint, face, or a security key — no password to type.
settings_passkey_error: The passkey operation could not be completed.
login_passkey_error: Passkey sign-in didn't complete. Try again, or use your password.
settings_apitoken_hint: Create tokens to let scripts, CI, and agents use the API on your behalf.
settings_apitoken_scope_all_hint: Grants every permission your account has.
settings_apitoken_created_hint: Copy it now — it won't be shown again.
settings_apitoken_none: You don't have any API tokens yet.
settings_apitoken_incomplete: Enter a name and select at least one scope.
settings_apitoken_error: The token operation could not be completed.
status_subsystem_database_desc: Application data — time entries, projects, customers, users.
status_subsystem_api_tokens_desc: Personal access tokens for programmatic API access.
status_subsystem_passkeys_mfa_desc: WebAuthn passkeys and TOTP two-factor secrets.
status_subsystem_mcp_desc: Model Context Protocol endpoint for coding agents (Streamable HTTP).
worklogsync_prefs_intro: Choose whether the nightly sync keeps your Jira worklogs and tracker entries in step, under your own Jira token.
worklogsync_prefs_enable_hint: The nightly sync reads and writes your own worklogs under your Jira token.
worklogsync_prefs_sync_all: Sync all worklogs I can access in Jira
worklogsync_prefs_sync_all_hint: Runs under your token and covers colleagues who don't use the tracker or haven't opted in. Only worklogs your Jira permissions let you see are synced.
worklogsync_prefs_error: Could not save your sync preferences.
worklogsync_prefs_none: You have no connected Jira ticket systems.
worklogsync_import_intro: Import your logged time from Jira into the tracker.
worklogsync_dryrun_note: Preview only — nothing has been imported yet.
